.with_target(true)\n277\t            .with_current_span(true)\n278\t            .with_span_list(false);\n279\t        registry()\n280\t            .with(filter)\n281\t )\n372\t            .await\n373\t            {\n374\t                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n375\t            }\n376\t        });\n377\t store configured)\");\n398\t    }\n399\t\n400\t    // Start drift reconciler background task (plan §13.5)\n401\t    // Uses the drift_reconciler from AppState which is already configured\n402\t    if reconciler started\");\n406\t            drift_reconciler.run().await;\n407\t            error!(\"drift reconciler exited unexpectedly\");\n408\t        });\n409\t    } else {\n410\t        info!(\"drift reconciler not available (no task store configured)\");\n411\t    }\n412\t\n413\t    // Start anti-entropy worker background task (plan §13.8)\n414\t    // Uses the anti_entropy_worker from AppState which is already configured\n415\t    if let Some(ref anti_entropy_worker) = state.admin.anti_entropy_worker {\n416\t        let anti_entropy_worker = anti_entropy_worker.clone();\n417\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n418\t            info!(\"anti-entropy worker started\");\n419\t            anti_entropy_worker.run().await;\n420\t            error!(\"anti-entropy worker exited unexpectedly\");\n421\t        });\n422\t    } else {\n423\t        info!(\"anti-entropy worker not available (disabled or no task store configured)\");\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    // Start Mode C worker background task (plan §14.5 Mode C)\n427\t    // Processes chunked background jobs (dump import, reshard backfill)\n428\t    if let Some(ref mode_c_worker) = state.admin.mode_c_worker {\n429\t        let mode_c_worker = mode_c_worker.clone();\n430\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n431\t            info!(\"Mode C worker started\");\n432\t            match mode_c_worker.run().await {\n433\t                Ok(()) => {\n434\t                    info!(\"Mode C worker exited cleanly\");\n435\t                }\n436\t                Err(e) => {\n437\t                    error!(\"Mode C worker exited unexpectedly: {}\", e);\n438\t                }\n439\t            }\n440\t        });\n441\t    } else {\n442\t        info!(\"Mode C worker not available (no task store configured)\");\n443\t    }\n444\t\n445\t    // Start peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5)\n446\t    // Periodically performs SRV lookups to discover peer pods\n447\t    if let Some(ref peer_discovery) = state.peer_discovery {\n448\t        let peer_discovery = peer_discovery.clone();\n449\t        let metrics = state.metrics.clone();\n450\t        let refresh_interval_s = config.peer_discovery.refresh_interval_s;\n451\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n452\t            let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(refresh_interval_s));\n453\t            info!(\n454\t                interval_s = refresh_interval_s,\n455\t                \"peer discovery refresh loop started\"\n456\t            );\n457\t            loop {\n458\t                interval.tick().await;\n459\t                match peer_discovery.refresh().await {\n460\t                    Ok(peer_set) => {\n461\t                        let count = peer_set.len() as u64;\n462\t                        info!(\n463\t                            peer_count = count,\n464\t                            \"peer discovery refresh completed\"\n465\t                        );\n466\t                        metrics.set_peer_pod_count(count);\n467\t                    }\n468\t                    Err(e) => {\n469\t                        error!(error = %e, \"peer discovery refresh failed\");\n470\t                    }\n471\t                }\n472\t            }\n473\t        });\n474\t    } else {\n475\t        info!(\"peer discovery disabled (not running in Kubernetes)\");\n476\t    }\n477\t\n478\t    // Start task registry TTL pruner background task (plan §4, Phase 3)\n479\t    // Runs on single-pod with advisory lock; Phase 6 §14.5 Mode A replaces with rendezvous\n480\t    if let Some(ref store) = state.admin.task_store {\n481\t        let store = store.clone();\n482\t        let pruner_config = config.task_registry.clone();\n483\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n484\t            // The pruner runs in its own thread via spawn_pruner\n485\t            let _pruner_handle = task_pruner::spawn_pruner(store, pruner_config);\n486\t            // The handle is dropped here only on process exit\n487\t            info!(\"task registry TTL pruner started\");\n488\t            // Keep this task alive forever\n489\t            std::future::pending::<()>().await;\n490\t        });\n491\t    } else {\n492\t        info!(\"task registry TTL pruner not available (no task store)\");\n493\t    }\n494\t\n495\t    // Start canary runner background task (plan §13.18)\n496\t    // Only enabled when canary_runner.enabled = true and Redis is available\n497\t    if config.canary_runner.enabled {\n498\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n499\t            let store: Arc<dyn TaskStore> = Arc::from(redis.clone());\n500\t            let canary_config = config.canary_runner.clone();\n501\t\n502\t            // Clone config values for the search executor\n503\t            let search_config = config.clone();\n504\t            let search_executor: miroir_core::canary::SearchExecutor = Arc::new(\n505\t                move |index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery| -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = miroir_core::error::Result<SearchResponse>> + Send>> {\n506\t                    let index_uid = index_uid.to_string();\n507\t                    let query = query.clone();\n508\t                    let config = search_config.clone();\n509\t\n510\t                    Box::pin(async move {\n511\t                        // For canary queries, we execute against the first available healthy node\n512\t                        let node_addresses: Vec<_> = config.nodes.iter()\n513\t                            .map(|n| n.address.clone())\n514\t                            .collect();\n515\t\n516\t                        for address in node_addresses {\n517\t                            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n518\t                                .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_millis(config.scatter.node_timeout_ms))\n519\t                                .build()\n520\t                            {\n521\t                                Ok(c) => c,\n522\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n523\t                            };\n524\t\n525\t                            let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n526\t\n527\t                            // Build the search request body\n528\t                            let mut body = match serde_json::to_value(&query) {\n529\t                                Ok(v) => v,\n530\t                                Err(e) => return Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e))),\n531\t                            };\n532\t\n533\t                            // Add limit to avoid large responses for canary queries\n534\t                            if !body.get(\"limit\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).is_some() {\n535\t                                body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(20);\n536\t                            }\n537\t\n538\t                            let response = match client.post(&url)\n539\t                                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", config.node_master_key))\n540\t                                .json(&body)\n541\t                                .send()\n542\t                                .await\n543\t                            {\n544\t                                Ok(r) => r,\n545\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n546\t                            };\n547\t\n548\t                            if response.status().is_success() {\n549\t                                if let Ok(text) = response.text().await {\n550\t                                    if let Ok(search_response) = serde_json::from_str::<SearchResponse>(&text) {\n551\t                                        return Ok(search_response);\n552\t                                    }\n553\t                                }\n554\t                            }\n555\t                        }\n556\t\n557\t                        // All nodes failed\n558\t                        Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::Topology(\n559\t                            \"All nodes failed for canary query\".to_string()\n560\t                        ))\n561\t                    })\n562\t                }\n563\t            );\n564\t\n565\t            // Create metrics emitter callback\n566\t            let metrics_for_canary = state.metrics.clone();\n567\t            let metrics_emitter: miroir_core::canary::MetricsEmitter = Arc::new(\n568\t                move |result| {\n569\t                    use miroir_core::canary::CanaryStatus;\n570\t                    let result_str = match result.status {\n571\t                        CanaryStatus::Passed => \"passed\",\n572\t                        CanaryStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n573\t                        CanaryStatus::Error => \"error\",\n574\t                    };\n575\t                    metrics_for_canary.inc_canary_runs(&result.canary_id, result_str);\n576\t                    metrics_for_canary.observe_canary_latency_ms(&result.canary_id, result.latency_ms as f64);\n577\t\n578\t                    for failure in &result.failed_assertions {\n579\t                        metrics_for_canary.inc_canary_assertion_failures(&result.canary_id, &failure.assertion_type);\n580\t                    }\n581\t                }\n582\t            );\n583\t\n584\t            // Create settings version checker callback\n585\t            let store_for_version = store.clone();\n586\t            let version_config = config.clone();\n587\t            let settings_version_checker: miroir_core::canary::SettingsVersionChecker = Arc::new(\n588\t                move |index_uid: &str| -> Option<i64> {\n589\t                    // Try to get the settings version from the task store\n590\t                    let node_ids: Vec<String> = version_config.nodes.iter()\n591\t                        .map(|n| n.id.clone())\n592\t                        .collect();\n593\t\n594\t                    let mut min_version: Option<i64> = None;\n595\t                    for node_id in node_ids {\n596\t                        if let Ok(Some(row)) = store_for_version.get_node_settings_version(index_uid, &node_id) {\n597\t                            match min_version {\n598\t                                None => min_version = Some(row.version),\n599\t                                Some(current) if row.version < current => min_version = Some(row.version),\n600\t                                _ => {}\n601\t                            }\n602\t                        }\n603\t                    }\n604\t                    min_version\n605\t                }\n606\t            );\n607\t\n608\t            // Create and start the canary runner\n609\t            let runner = CanaryRunner::new(\n610\t                store,\n611\t                canary_config.max_concurrent_canaries as usize,\n612\t                canary_config.run_history_per_canary as usize,\n613\t                search_executor,\n614\t                metrics_emitter,\n615\t                settings_version_checker,\n616\t            );\n617\t\n618\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n619\t                info!(\"canary runner started\");\n620\t                if let Err(e) = runner.start().await {\n621\t                    error!(\"canary runner exited: {}\", e);\n622\t                }\n623\t            });\n624\t        } else {\n625\t            info!(\"canary runner enabled but Redis not available - skipping\");\n626\t        }\n627\t    }\n628\t\n629\t    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n630\t    let app = Router::new()\n631\t        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n632\t        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n633\t        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n634\t        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n635\t        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n636\t        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n637\t        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n638\t        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n639\t        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n640\t        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n641\t        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n642\t        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n643\t        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n644\t        // 2. auth_middleware\n645\t        // 3. Extension layers\n646\t        // 4. session_pinning_middleware - extracts X-Miroir-Session header\n647\t        // 5. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n648\t        // 6. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n649\t        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n650\t        //\n651\t        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n652\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n653\t            TelemetryState {\n654\t                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n655\t                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n656\t            },\n657\t            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n658\t        ))\n659\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n660\t            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n661\t        ))\n662\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n663\t            middleware::session_pinning_middleware,\n664\t        ))\n665\t        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n666\t            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n667\t        ))\n668\t        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n669\t        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n670\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n671\t            state.auth.clone(),\n672\t            auth::auth_middleware,\n673\t        ))\n674\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n675\t            auth::CsrfState {\n676\t                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n677\t                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n678\t            },\n679\t            auth::csrf_middleware,\n680\t        ))\n681\t        .with_state(state.clone());\n682\t\n683\t    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n684\t        .parse()\n685\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n686\t    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n687\t\n688\t    info!(\n689\t        main_addr = %main_addr,\n690\t        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n691\t        \"listening\"\n692\t    );\n693\t\n694\t    // Create listeners\n695\t    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n696\t    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n697\t\n698\t    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n699\t    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n700\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n701\t\n702\t    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n703\t    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n704\t    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n705\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n706\t\n707\t    // Run both servers concurrently\n708\t    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n709\t        main_server,\n710\t        metrics_server\n711\t    );\n712\t\n713\t    // Check for errors\n714\t    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n715\t        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n716\t    }\n717\t    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n718\t        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n719\t    }\n720\t\n721\t    Ok(())\n722\t}\n723\t\n724\t/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n725\t///\n726\t/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n727\t/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\n728\tasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n729\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n730\t        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n731\t    ));\n732\t\n733\t    loop {\n734\t        interval.tick().await;\n735\t\n736\t        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n737\t        let mut all_healthy = true;\n738\t\n739\t        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n740\t        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n741\t\n742\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n743\t            // Get current node status\n744\t            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n745\t\n746\t            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n747\t            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n748\t                continue;\n749\t            }\n750\t\n751\t            // Get node address\n752\t            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n753\t                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n754\t                None => {\n755\t                    all_healthy = false;\n756\t                    continue;\n757\t                }\n758\t            };\n759\t\n760\t            // Try to reach the node\n761\t            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n762\t                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n763\t                .build()\n764\t            {\n765\t                Ok(c) => c,\n766\t                Err(_) => {\n767\t                    all_healthy = false;\n768\t                    continue;\n769\t                }\n770\t            };\n771\t\n772\t            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n773\t            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n774\t\n775\t            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n776\t                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n777\t                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n778\t                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n779\t                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n780\t                }\n781\t            } else {\n782\t                all_healthy = false;\n783\t            }\n784\t        }\n785\t\n786\t        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n787\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n788\t            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n789\t            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n790\t        }\n791\t\n792\t        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n793\t        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n794\t\n795\t        // Update task registry size gauge\n796\t        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n797\t        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n798\t\n799\t        // Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus\n800\t        state.sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus().await;\n801\t\n802\t        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n803\t        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n804\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n805\t        }\n806\t\n807\t        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n808\t        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n809\t\n810\t        // Update §13.6 session pinning metrics\n811\t        state.session_manager.update_metrics(|count| {\n812\t            state.metrics.set_session_active_count(count as u64);\n813\t        });\n814\t\n815\t        // Prune expired sessions (plan §13.6)\n816\t        let pruned = state.session_manager.prune_expired().await;\n817\t        if pruned > 0 {\n818\t            info!(\n819\t                pruned_count = pruned,\n820\t                \"pruned expired sessions\"\n821\t            );\n822\t        }\n823\t    }\n824\t}\n825\t\n826\t/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n827\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n828\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n829\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n830\t    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n831\t    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n832\t\n833\t    // Per-node shard count\n834\t    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n835\t        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n836\t\n837\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n838\t        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n839\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n840\t            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n841\t                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n842\t            );\n843\t            for node_id in &assigned {\n844\t                let healthy = node_map\n845\t                    .get(node_id)\n846\t                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n847\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n848\t                if healthy {\n849\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n850\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n851\t                }\n852\t            }\n853\t        }\n854\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n855\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n856\t        } else {\n857\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n858\t        }\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n862\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n863\t    } else {\n864\t        1.0\n865\t    };\n866\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n867\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n868\t\n869\t    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n870\t        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n871\t    }\n872\t}\n873\t\n874\t/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n875\t///\n876\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n877\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n878\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n879\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n880\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n881\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n882\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n883\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n884\t\n885\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n886\t        if max > 0 {\n887\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n888\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n889\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n890\t        }\n891\t    }\n892\t\n893\t    // ── CPU throttling ──\n894\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n895\t    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n896\t        for line in contents.lines() {\n897\t            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n898\t                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n899\t                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n900\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n901\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n902\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n903\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n904\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n905\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n906\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n907\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n908\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n909\t                }\n910\t            }\n911\t        }\n912\t    }\n913\t\n914\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n915\t    // Peer pod count is now set by peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5).\n916\t    // Leader election is not yet implemented (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n917\t    // Owned shards count will be set by Mode A rendezvous (plan §14.5).\n918\t    metrics.set_leader(\"global\", true);\n919\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n920\t}\n921\t\n922\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n923\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n924\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n925\t        .ok()\n926\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n927\t}\n928\t\n929\t/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\n930\tasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n931\t    let ctrl_c = async {\n932\t        signal::ctrl_c()\n933\t            .await\n934\t            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C available - skipping\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n        // 2. auth_middleware\n        // 3. Extension layers\n        // 4. session_pinning_middleware - extracts X-Miroir-Session header\n        // 5. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n        // 6. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n        //\n        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            TelemetryState {\n                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n            },\n            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::session_pinning_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state.auth.clone(),\n            auth::auth_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            auth::CsrfState {\n                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n            },\n            auth::csrf_middleware,\n        ))\n        .with_state(state.clone());\n\n    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n\n    info!(\n        main_addr = %main_addr,\n        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n        \"listening\"\n    );\n\n    // Create listeners\n    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n\n    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Run both servers concurrently\n    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n        main_server,\n        metrics_server\n    );\n\n    // Check for errors\n    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n///\n/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\nasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n    ));\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n        let mut all_healthy = true;\n\n        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            // Get current node status\n            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n\n            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address\n            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n                None => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Try to reach the node\n            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n\n            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n                }\n            } else {\n                all_healthy = false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n        }\n\n        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n\n        // Update task registry size gauge\n        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n\n        // Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus\n        state.sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus().await;\n\n        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n        }\n\n        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n\n        // Update §13.6 session pinning metrics\n        state.session_manager.update_metrics(|count| {\n            state.metrics.set_session_active_count(count as u64);\n        });\n\n        // Prune expired sessions (plan §13.6)\n        let pruned = state.session_manager.prune_expired().await;\n        if pruned > 0 {\n            info!(\n                pruned_count = pruned,\n                \"pruned expired sessions\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\nfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n\n    // Per-node shard count\n    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n            );\n            for node_id in &assigned {\n                let healthy = node_map\n                    .get(node_id)\n                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n                if healthy {\n                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if has_healthy_replica {\n            healthy_shards += 1;\n        } else {\n            degraded_shards += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n    } else {\n        1.0\n    };\n    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n\n    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n///\n/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\nfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    // ── Memory pressure ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n\n    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n        if max > 0 {\n            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── CPU throttling ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n        for line in contents.lines() {\n            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n    // Peer pod count is now set by peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5).\n    // Leader election is not yet implemented (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n    // Owned shards count will be set by Mode A rendezvous (plan §14.5).\n    metrics.set_leader(\"global\", true);\n    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n}\n\n/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\nfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n}\n\n/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\nasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n    let ctrl_c = async {\n        signal::ctrl_c()\n            .await\n            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    let terminate = async {\n        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n            .recv()\n            .await;\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n\n    tokio::select! {\n        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n        _ = terminate => {},\n    }\n\n    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n\n    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n    otel::shutdown_otel();\n}\n","numLines":958,"startLine":1,"totalLines":958}}}

Keys management endpoints: CRUD with broadcast to all nodes.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.4:\n//! - `POST /keys` — create key on every node (all-or-nothing)\n//! - `PATCH /keys/{key}` — update key on every node (sequential with rollback)\n//! - `DELETE /keys/{key}` — delete key on every node (all-or-nothing)\n//! - `GET /keys` — list keys (proxy to first node)\n//! - `GET /keys/{key}` — get key (proxy to first node)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path};\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::{get, post};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::config::Config;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse crate::routes::indexes::MeilisearchClient;\n\n/// Collect all node addresses from config.\nfn all_node_addresses(config: &Config) -> Vec<String> {\n    config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect()\n}\n\n/// Try to forward a Meilisearch error; fall back to a Miroir error.\nfn forward_or_miroir(_status: u16, body: &str, fallback_msg: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n    if let Some(meili_err) = MeilisearchError::forwarded(body) {\n        meili_err\n    } else {\n        MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, fallback_msg)\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", post(create_key_handler).get(list_keys_handler))\n        .route(\n            \"/:key\",\n            get(get_key_handler).patch(update_key_handler).delete(delete_key_handler),\n        )\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// POST /keys — create key (all-or-nothing broadcast)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn create_key_handler(\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.post_raw(address, \"/keys\", &body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n                created_on.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                // Rollback: delete key on all previously created nodes\n                rollback_delete_key(&client, &body, &created_on).await;\n                let msg = format!(\n                    \"key creation failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n                    address, status, text\n                );\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                rollback_delete_key(&client, &body, &created_on).await;\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"key creation failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"status\": \"created\"}))))\n}\n\n/// Rollback by deleting the key from nodes where it was successfully created.\nasync fn rollback_delete_key(\n    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n    body: &Value,\n    nodes: &[String],\n) {\n    // Try to get the key UID from the creation body or extract it\n    let key_or_name = body\n        .get(\"uid\")\n        .or(body.get(\"name\"))\n        .or(body.get(\"key\"))\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .unwrap_or(\"\");\n\n    if key_or_name.is_empty() {\n        tracing::warn!(\"key rollback: cannot determine key identifier for rollback\");\n        return;\n    }\n\n    for address in nodes {\n        let path = format!(\"/keys/{}\", key_or_name);\n        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n            Ok(_) => tracing::info!(node = %address, \"key rollback: deleted key\"),\n            Err(e) => tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"key rollback: failed to delete key\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// PATCH /keys/{key} — update key (sequential broadcast with rollback)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn update_key_handler(\n    Path(key): Path<String>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n    let path = format!(\"/keys/{}\", key);\n\n    // Snapshot current key state from all nodes\n    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, Value)> = Vec::new();\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.get_raw(address, &path).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                let snapshot: Value = serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n                snapshots.push((address.clone(), snapshot));\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(\n                    status,\n                    &text,\n                    &format!(\"failed to snapshot key on {}: HTTP {}\", address, status),\n                ));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"failed to snapshot key on {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply update sequentially\n    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    for (address, _snapshot) in &snapshots {\n        match client.patch_raw(address, &path, &body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n                applied.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                rollback_key_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n                let msg = format!(\n                    \"key update failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n                    address, status, text\n                );\n                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                rollback_key_update(&client, &path, &snapshots, &applied).await;\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n                    format!(\"key update failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"status\": \"updated\"}))))\n}\n\n/// Rollback key updates by restoring pre-change snapshots.\nasync fn rollback_key_update(\n    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n    path: &str,\n    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n    applied: &[String],\n) {\n    for address in applied {\n        if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == address) {\n            match client.patch_raw(address, path, snapshot).await {\n                Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {\n                    tracing::info!(node = %address, \"key rollback succeeded\");\n                }\n                Ok((status, _text)) => {\n                    tracing::error!(node = %address, status, \"key rollback failed\");\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"key rollback failed\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DELETE /keys/{key} — delete key (all-or-nothing broadcast)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync fn delete_key_handler(\n    Path(key): Path<String>,\n    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n    let path = format!(\"/keys/{}\", key);\n    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n    let mut errors: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {} — {}\", address, status, text));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", address, e));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !errors.is_empty() && first_response.is_none() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            format!(\"key deletion failed on all nodes: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    if !errors.is_empty() {\n        // Hash the key identifier for correlation without logging the raw value (plan §10: no PII).\n        let key_hash = {\n            use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n            let mut h = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n            key.hash(&mut h);\n            format!(\"{:016x}\", h.finish())\n        };\n        tracing::warn!(key_hash = %key_hash, errors = errors.len(), \"key deletion partially failed\");\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"status\": \"deleted\"}))))\n}\n\n// && status < 300 {\n        Ok(Json(serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap_or(Value::Null)))\n    } else {\n        Err(StatusCode::from_u16(status).unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))\n       address: &str,\n222\t        index_uid: &str,\n223\t    ) -> Result<f64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {\n224\t        let url = format!(\n225\t            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents\",\n226\t }\n249\t\n250\t            let mut total_length = 0u64;\n251\t            let mut field_count = 0u64;\n252\t\n253\t            for doc in docs {\n254\t                if let Some(obj) = u64;\n258\t                            field_count += 1;\n259\t                        }\n260\t                    }\n261\t                }\n262\t            }\n263\t\n264\t            if field_count > 0 {\n265\t                return Ok(total_length as f64 / field_count as f64);\n266\t            }\n267\t        }\n268\t\n269\t        Ok(500.0)\n270\t    }\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/// Collect all fingerprint (SHA256) of settings as canonical JSON.\n279\t///\n280\t/// Canonical JSON sorts object keys to ensure consistent fingerprints\n281\t/// regardless of key ordering in the input.\n282\tfn fingerprint_settings(settings: &Value) -> String {\n283\t    // Canonicalize: sort object keys, no extra whitespace.\n284\t    let canonical = if settings.is_object() {\n285\t        if let Some(obj) = settings.as_object() {\n286\t            // Collect and sort keys.\n287\t            let mut sorted_entries: Vec<_> = obj.iter().collect();\n288\t            sorted_entries.sort_by_key(|&(k, _)| k);\n289\t            // Reconstruct as a Map with sorted keys.\n290\t            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n291\t            for (key, value) in sorted_entries {\n292\t                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());\n293\t            }\n294\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_default()\n295\t        } else {\n296\t            serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n297\t        }\n298\t    } else {\n299\t        serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n300\t    };\n301\t\n302\t    // SHA256 hash.\n303\t    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n304\t    hasher.update(canonical.as_bytes());\n305\t    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n306\t}\n307\t\n308\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n309\twhere\n310\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n311\t{\n312\t    Router::new()\n313\t        .route(\"/\", post(create_index_handler).get(list_indexes_handler))\n314\t        .route(\n315\t            \"/:index\",\n316\t            get(get_index_handler)\n317\t                .patch(update_index_handler)\n318\t                .delete(delete_index_handler),\n319\t        )\n320\t        .route(\"/:index/stats\", get(get_index_stats_handler))\n321\t        .route(\n322\t            \"/:index/settings\",\n323\t get(get_settings_subpath_handler).patch(update_settings_subpath_handler),\n328\t        )\n329\t        .route(\"/:index/_preflight\", post(preflight_handler))\n330\t        .nest(\"/:index/documents\", documents::router::<S>())\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n334\t// POST /indexes — create index (broadcast + _miroir_shard)\n335\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n336\t\n337\tasync fn create_index_handler(\n338\t    Extension(_state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n339\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n340\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n341\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n342\t    let uid = body\n343\t        .get(\"uid\")\n344\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n345\t        .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(\n346\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n347\t            \"index creation
