Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n            origin: None, // Client write\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n        origin: None, // Client write\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            // Compute SHA256 hash of the request body\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            // Check cache\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    // Idempotency hit: return cached mtask ID\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0, // No degraded groups for cached response\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Cache miss - proceed with processing\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    // Key exists but body hash differs\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Other error - log but proceed (best-effort caching)\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with write\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n        Some(\n            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64\n        )\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n\n        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 7.6. Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            // Update cache size metric\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete by filter\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"pk\": \"other\", \"key\": \"another\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    // Reserved field validation tests (P2.9)\n    //\n    // Tests the reserved field matrix per plan §5:\n    // - `_miroir_shard`: Always reserved (unconditional)\n    // - `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n\n    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n        assert_eq!(err.error_type, miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_updated_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_expires_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_non_miroir_fields_allowed() {\n        // Non-reserved _miroir_ fields are allowed\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\", \"_miroir_metadata\": {\"key\": \"val\"}});\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_custom\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_metadata\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test matrix of all reserved field combinations per plan §5 table.\n    ///\n    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n    /// | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (anti_entropy) |\n    /// | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (ttl) |\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix() {\n        struct TestCase {\n            doc: Value,\n            description: &'static str,\n            has_shard: bool,\n            has_updated_at: bool,\n            has_expires_at: bool,\n        }\n\n        let test_cases = vec![\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\"}),\n                description: \"clean document should pass\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1}),\n                description: \"_miroir_shard always rejected\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_updated_at allowed when anti_entropy disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_expires_at\": \"2024-12-31T23:59:59Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_expires_at allowed when ttl.disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: true,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\"}),\n                description: \"non-reserved _miroir_ fields allowed\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"multiple reserved fields present\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        for tc in test_cases {\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), tc.has_shard, \"{}: shard check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some(), tc.has_updated_at, \"{}: updated_at check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").is_some(), tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n        );\n\n        // Verify Meilisearch-compatible error shape: {message, code, type, link}\n        let json = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n        assert!(json[\"message\"].is_string());\n        assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n        assert_eq!(json[\"message\"], \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_all_fields_present() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_updated_at`\",\n        );\n\n        // Verify all required fields are present\n        assert!(!err.message.is_empty());\n        assert!(!err.code.is_empty());\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert!(err.link.is_some());\n        assert!(err.link.unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_orchestrator_shard_injection_flow() {\n        // Simulate the orchestrator injection flow:\n        // 1. Client sends document WITHOUT _miroir_shard\n        // 2. Validation passes (no _miroir_shard present)\n        // 3. Orchestrator injects _miroir_shard for routing\n        // 4. Write proceeds with injected field\n\n        let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n        assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"client doc should not have _miroir_shard\");\n\n        // Simulate orchestrator injection (happens AFTER validation at line 279-290)\n        let mut doc_with_shard = client_doc.clone();\n        doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"] = json!(5);\n\n        assert_eq!(doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"], 5, \"orchestrator should inject _miroir_shard\");\n        assert!(doc_with_shard.get(\"id\").is_some(), \"primary key should still be present\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_validation_order() {\n        // _miroir_shard is checked BEFORE primary key validation (per acceptance criteria)\n        // This ensures clients can't bypass validation by including both fields\n\n        let doc_with_shard_no_pk = json!({\"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"name\": \"No PK\"});\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc_with_shard_no_pk.get(\"id\").is_none());\n\n        // The validation should catch _miroir_shard first, not missing primary key\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    }\n\n    // P2.9: Complete reserved field matrix tests\n    //\n    // Matrix cells per plan §5:\n    // | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    // |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n    // | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED        | REJECTED       |\n    // | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED    | REJECTED (AE)  |\n    // | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED    | REJECTED (TTL) |\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard: Always reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_updated_at_rejected_when_ae_enabled() {\n        // _miroir_updated_at: Rejected when anti_entropy.enabled: true\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_updated_at` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\",\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_expires_at_rejected_when_ttl_enabled() {\n        // _miroir_expires_at: Rejected when ttl.enabled: true\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_expires_at` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\",\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_updated_at_allowed_when_ae_disabled() {\n        // _miroir_updated_at: Allowed when anti_entropy.enabled: false\n        // When disabled, client values pass through end-to-end\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"});\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc.get(\"id\").is_some());\n        // No validation error would be raised in this case\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix_expires_at_allowed_when_ttl_disabled() {\n        // _miroir_expires_at: Allowed when ttl.enabled: false\n        // When disabled, client values pass through end-to-end\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_expires_at\": }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_orchestrator_updated_at_stamping_when_ae_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, orchestrator stamps _miroir_updated_at\n        // This test verifies the stamping logic (plan §13.8)\n\n        let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n        assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_none(), \"client doc should not have _miroir_updated_at\");\n\n        // Simulate orchestrator stamping (happens AFTER validation)\n        let mut doc_with_timestamp = client_doc.clone();\n        let now_ms = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_millis() as u64;\n        doc_with_timestamp[\"_miroir_updated_at\"] assert_eq!(doc_with_timestamp[\"_miroir_updated_at\"], now_ms, \"timestamp should be current ms since epoch\");\n        assert!(doc_with_timestamp.get(\"id\").is_some(), \"primary key should still be ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n251\t\n252\t/// Test: numberOfDocuments is divided by RG*RF to get logical count.\n253\t#[test]\n254\tfn test_stats_logical_doc_count() {\n255\t    let rg = 2u64;\n256\t    let rf = 1u64;\n257\t    let divisor = rg * rf;\n258\t\n259\t    // Simulate: 3 nodes each reporting 100 docs\n260\t    // RG=2, RF=1: nodes 0,1 in group 0, node 2 in group 1\n261\t    // Total raw = 300, logical = 300 / 2 = 150\n262\t    let total_docs: u64 = 300;\n263\t    let logical = total_docs / divisor;\n264\t    assert_eq!(logical, 150);\n265\t\n266\t    // Simulate: 2 nodes, RG=1, RF=1: both in same group\n267\t    // Total raw = 200, divisor = 1, logical = 200\n268\t    let rg1 = 1u64;\n269\t    let rf1 = 1u64;\n270\t    let logical_rg1 = 200u64 / (rg1 * rf1);\n271\t    assert_eq!(logical_rg1, 200);\n272\t}\n273\t\n274\t/// Test: fieldDistribution is summed per-field across nodes.\n275\t#[test]\n276\tfn test_field_distribution_merge() {\n277\t    use std::collections::HashMap;\n278\t\n279\t    let mut field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> =          if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n286\t                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n287\t            }\n288\t        }\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    // Node 2\n292\t    let fd2 = json!({\"title\": 150, \"body\": 250, \"tags\": 50});\n293\t    if let Some(obj) = fd2.as_object() {\n294\t        for (field, count) in obj {\n295\t            if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n296\t                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n297\t            }\n298\t        }\n299\t    }\n300\t\n301\t    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"title\").unwrap_or(&0), 250);\n302\t    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"body\").unwrap_or(&0), 450);\n303\t    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"tags\").unwrap_or(&0), 50);\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n307\t// Settings sequential Tests:\n//! - POST /indexes creates on every node; failure on any node rolls back\n//! - _miroir_shard is in filterableAttributes after creation\n//! - GET /indexes/{uid}/stats numberOfDocuments = broadcast with rollback\n//! - POST /keys creates on every node; failure rolls back\n//! - DELETE /keys/{key} broadcasts to all nodes\n\nuse miroir_core::config::{Config, MiroirConfig, NodeConfig};\nuse miroir_proxy::routes::indexes::MeilisearchClient;\nuse serde_json::json;\n\nfn make_config(node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> MiroirConfig {\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeConfig> = node_addresses\n        .into_iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, addr)| NodeConfig {\n            id: format!(\"node-{i}\"),\n            address: addr,\n            replica_group: 0,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirConfig {\n        master_key: \"test-master-key\".into(),\n        node_master_key: \"test-node-master-key\".into(),\n        shards: 64,\n        replication_factor: 1,\n        replica_groups: 1,\n        nodes,\n        ..Default::default()\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_config_rg2(node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> MiroirConfig {\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeConfig> = node_addresses\n        .into_iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, addr)| NodeConfig {\n            id: format!(\"node-{i}\"),\n            address: addr,\n            replica_group: (i % 2) as u32,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirConfig {\n        master_key: \"test-master-key\".into(),\n        node_master_key: \"test-node-master-key\".into(),\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// POST /indexes — create with rollback\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: Creating an index sends POST /indexes to every configured node.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_create_index_broadcasts_to_all_nodes() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut first_response: Option<serde_json::Value> = None;\n    let mut all_ok = true;\n\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n                created_on.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            _ => {\n                all_ok = false;\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    assert!(all_ok, \"all nodes should accept index creation\");\n    assert_eq!(created_on.len(), 2);\n\n    mock1.assert_async().await;\n    mock2.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n/// Test: If the second node fails during index creation, the first node's index is rolled back.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_create_index_rollback_on_failure() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Node 1: create succeeds\n    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Node 2: create fails\n    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .with_status(500)\n        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"internal error\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Rollback: delete on node 1\n    let rollback1 = server1.mock(\"DELETE\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n            Ok((status, _)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                created_on.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                // Rollback\n                for addr in &created_on {\n                    let _ = client.delete_raw(addr, \"/indexes/test-idx\").await;\n                }\n                assert!(created_on.len() == 1, \"first node should have been created before failure\");\n                break;\n            }\n            Err(_) => break,\n        }\n    }\n\n    mock1.assert_async().await;\n    mock2.assert_async().await;\n    rollback1.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// _miroir_shard in filterableAttributes\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: After creating an index, _miroir_shard is in filterableAttributes.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_miroir_shard_in_filterable_attributes() {\n    let mut server = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    let mock = server.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // GET settings returns current filterableAttributes\n    let get_settings = server.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"status\"], \"sortableAttributes\": []}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // PATCH settings should include both \"status\" and \"_miroir_shard\"\n    let patch_settings = }).to_string()))\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(vec![server.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    // Step 1: Create index\n    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n    let (status, _) = client.post_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes\", &body).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n\n    // Step 2: Read current settings and merge _miroir_shard\n    let mut merged_attrs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = vec![json!(\"_miroir_shard\")];\n    if let Ok((s, text)) = client.get_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\").await {\n        if s >= 200 && s < 300 {\n            if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&text) {\n                if let Some(existing) = settings.get(\"filterableAttributes\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n                    for attr in existing {\n                        let attr_str = attr.as_str().unwrap_or(\"\");\n                        if attr_str != \"_miroir_shard\" && !attr_str.is_empty() {\n                            merged_attrs.push(attr.clone());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 3: PATCH with merged filterableAttributes\n    let patch = json!({\"filterableAttributes\": merged_attrs});\n    let (status, _) = client.patch_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\", &patch).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n\n Stats aggregation — logical document count\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: numberOfDocuments is divided by RG*RF to get logical count.\n#[test]\nfn test_stats_logical_doc_count() {\n    let rg = 2u64;\n    let rf = 1u64;\n    let divisor = rg * rf;\n\n    // Simulate: 3 nodes each reporting 100 docs\n    // RG=2, RF=1: nodes 0,1 in group 0, node 2 in group 1\n    // Total raw = 300, logical = 300 / 2 = 150\n    let total_docs: u64 = 300;\n    let logical = total_docs / divisor;\n    assert_eq!(logical, 150);\n\n    // Simulate: 2 nodes, RG=1, RF=1: both in same group\n    // Total raw = 200, divisor = 1, logical = 200\n    let rg1 = 1u64;\n    let rf1 = 1u64;\n    let logical_rg1 = 200u64 / (rg1 * rf1);\n    assert_eq!(logical_rg1, 200);\n}\n\n/// Test: fieldDistribution is summed per-field across nodes.\n#[test]\nfn test_field_distribution_merge() {\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    let mut field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Node 1\n    let fd1 = json!({\"title\": 100, \"body\": 200});\n    if let Some(obj) = fd1.as_object() {\n        for (field, count) in obj {\n            if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Node 2\n    let fd2 = json!({\"title\": 150, \"body\": 250, \"tags\": 50});\n    if let Some(obj) = fd2.as_object() {\n        for (field, count) in obj {\n            if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"title\").unwrap_or(&0), 250);\n    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"body\").unwrap_or(&0), 450);\n    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"tags\").unwrap_or(&0), 50);\n}\n\n// test_settings_broadcast_rollback() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Snapshot current settings from node 1\n    let get1 = server1.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Snapshot from node 2\n    let get2 = server2.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // PATCH succeeds on node 1\n    let patch1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": .with_status(500)\n        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"internal error\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Rollback: restore original settings        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n1149\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n1150\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n1151\t        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n1152\t            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n1153\t        })?)\n1154\t    }\n1155\t\n1156\t    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n1157\t        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n1158\t    }\n1159\t\n1160\t    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n1161\t        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n1162\t    }\n1163\t}\n1164\t\n1165\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n1166\t///\n1167\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n1168\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n1169\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n1170\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n1171\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n1172\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n1173\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n1174\t\n1175\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n1176\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1177\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n1178\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n1179\t\n1180\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n1181\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n1182\t}\n1183\t\n1184\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n1185\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n1186\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n1187\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n1188\t}\n1189\t\n1190\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n1191\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n1192\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n1193\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1194\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n1195\t    }\n1196\t\n1197\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n1198\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n1199\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1200\t        }\n1201\t    }\n1202\t}\n1203\t\n1204\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n1205\t///\n1206\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n1207\t/// is accurately decremented.\n1208\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n1209\t    metrics: Metrics,\n1210\t    request_id: String,\n1211\t}\n1212\t\n1213\timpl InFlightGuard {\n1214\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n1215\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n1216\t        tracing::trace!(\n1217\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1218\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1219\t            \"request started\"\n1220\t        );\n1221\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n1222\t    }\n1223\t}\n1224\t\n1225\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n1226\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n1227\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n1228\t        tracing::trace!(\n1229\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n1230\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1231\t            \"request completed\"\n1232\t        );\n1233\t    }\n1234\t}\n1235\t\n1236\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n1237\t///\n1238\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n1239\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n1240\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n1241\t    request\n1242\t        .extensions()\n1243\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n1244\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n1245\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n1246\t        .to_string()\n1247\t}\n1248\t\n1249\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n1250\t///\n1251\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n1252\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n1253\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n1254\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n1255\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n1256\t    mut req: Request,\n1257\t    next: Next,\n1258\t) -> Response {\n1259\t    let start = Instant::now();\n1260\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n1261\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n1262\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n1263\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n1264\t\n1265\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n1266\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1267\t    let request_id = req\n1268\t        .headers()\n1269\t        .get_request_id()\n1270\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1271\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1272\t\n1273\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1274\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1275\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1276\t    let span = info_span!(\n1277\t        \"request\",\n1278\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1279\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1280\t        method = %method,\n1281\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1282\t    );\n1283\t\n1284\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1285\t\n1286\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1287\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1288\t\n1289\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1290\t\n1291\t    drop(in_flight);\n1292\t\n1293\t    let status = response.status();\n1294\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1295\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1296\t\n1297\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1298\t    metrics\n1299\t        .request_duration\n1300\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1301\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1302\t    metrics\n1303\t        .requests_total\n1304\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1305\t        .inc();\n1306\t\n1307\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1308\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1309\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1310\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1311\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1312\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1313\t        tracing::error!(\n1314\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1315\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1316\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1317\t            message = %message,\n1318\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1319\t            status = status_u16,\n1320\t            method = %method,\n1321\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1322\t        );\n1323\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1324\t        tracing::warn!(\n1325\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1326\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1327\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1328\t            message = %message,\n1329\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1330\t            status = status_u16,\n1331\t            method = %method,\n1332\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1333\t        );\n1334\t    } else {\n1335\t        tracing::info!(\n1336\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1337\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1338\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1339\t            message = %message,\n1340\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1341\t            status = status_u16,\n1342\t            method = %method,\n1343\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1344\t        );\n1345\t    }\n1346\t\n1347\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1348\t    let mut response = response;\n1349\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1350\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1351\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1352\t        }\n1353\t    }\n1354\t\n1355\t    response\n1356\t}\n1357\t\n1358\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1359\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1360\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1361\t}\n1362\t\n1363\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1364\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> Response {\n1365\t    let body = match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1366\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1367\t        Err(e) => {\n1368\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1369\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1370\t        }\n1371\t    };\n1372\t\n1373\t    let mut response = Response::new(axum::body::Body::from(body));\n1374\t    response.headers_mut().insert(\n1375\t        \"content-type\",\n1376\t        HeaderValue::from_static(\"text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8\"),\n1377\t    );\n1378\t    response\n1379\t}\n1380\t\n1381\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1382\timpl Metrics {\n1383\t    // ── Request metrics ──\n1384\t\n1385\t    pub fn record_request_duration(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n1386\t        self.request_duration.with_label_values(&[method, path_template, &status.to_string()]).observe(duration_secs);\n1387\t    }\n1388\t\n1389\t    pub fn inc_requests_total(&self, method: &str, }\n1876\t}\n1877\t\n1878\t#[cfg(test)]\n1879\tmod tests {\n1880\t    use super::*;\n1881\t\n1882\t    #[test]\n1883\t    fn test_request_id_generation() {\n1884\t        // Generate multiple IDs to verify format\n1885\t        for _ in 0..10 {\n1886\t            let id = generate_request_id();\n1887\t\n1888\t            // IDs should be 16 hex chars (64-bit hash)\n1889\t            assert_eq!(id.len(), 16);\n1890\t\n1891\t            // IDs should be hexadecimal\n1892\t            assert!(id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n1893\t        }\n1894\t\n1895\t        // Test that different UUID prefixes produce different IDs\n1896\t        let id1 = generate_request_id();\n1897\t        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n1898\t        let id2 = generate_request_id();\n1899\t        // In production, time ensures uniqueness; test just verifies format above\n1900\t        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 16);\n1901\t        assert_eq!(id2.len(), 16);\n1902\t    }\n1903\t\n1904\t    #[test]\n1905\t    fn test_metrics_creation() {\n1906\t        // Default config has all §13 features enabled\n1907\t        let metrics = Metrics::new(&MiroirConfig::default());\n1908\t\n1909\t        // Add some sample data to ensure metrics show up in output\n1910\t        metrics.request_duration.with_label_values(&[\"GET\",
